purpose: Prepare demo-specific Distill ribbons for real local runs and viewer-ready measured artifacts without allowing unmeasured claims.
shared_ribbon_principles: Baseline and Distill Accuracy must share the same seeded protocol, reference source, and scoring contract.
Distill may improve only by acting through measured support evidence and must record every accept, correction, backtrack, tighten, stop, or refusal action.
Projected corrections are preferred when diagnostics show residual concentration in soft or defect coordinates; stiff-axis drift stays budgeted and visible.
Energy improvements are not wins if they create force explosions, stress explosions, nonphysical jumps, cell collapse, or wrong-equilibrium failures.
The viewer renders measured trajectories and scored overlays only; planned scene descriptions are work instructions, not visual evidence.

science_contract.primary_question: Can a same-material defect-aware ribbon improve Ni vacancy diffusion and local equilibrium observables without damaging the EAM-home-turf bulk mechanics anchor?

required_sources: NIST or OpenKIM Ni EAM model record selected as classical baseline
published or accepted migration-barrier or hop-rate reference when claiming diffusion

support_plan.support_family: non-overlapping Ni bulk, surface, vacancy-shell, and near-defect perturbations
support_plan.eval_family: held-out vacancy MD or offset-relaxation trajectories with sealed seeds
support_plan.leakage_guard: hash structure graph, material root, defect site id, seed, and perturbation protocol

allowed_correction_coordinates: soft local displacement complement from spectral diagnostics
stiff_axes_to_preserve: nearest-neighbor shell ordering outside the vacancy region
refusal_triggers: vacancy site annihilates or changes topology outside protocol
bulk lattice drift exceeds stiff-axis budget

phase_0_preflight: Lock the Ni EAM and vacancy reference source.
Generate one small local vacancy trajectory with baseline only.
Run Distill in shadow mode over the same frames and record decisions.
phase_1_local_pair: Run MACE, ORB, SevenNet, and CHGNet where imports are clean.
Compare baseline and Distill Accuracy on the same seeded protocol.
Promote only if no bulk-anchor regression is observed.
phase_2_cloud: Upload the locked policy and fixture.
Run a small Cloud Run canary before any expanded campaign.
Publish paired artifacts and viewer frames after validation.

viewer_contract.scene_rule: Render only measured atom positions, measured vacancy site markers, scored local-shell distortion, and real intervention timestamps.

science_contract.primary_question: Can a projected ribbon improve an hcp shear or stacking-fault path while preserving the stiff lattice axes and refusing unstable cells?

required_sources: published hcp Mg stacking-fault energy or gamma-surface reference
OpenKIM or NIST Mg EAM/MEAM model record when used as a classical baseline
locked elastic constants used only as consistency checks

support_plan.support_family: non-overlapping hcp Mg finite-strain, basal shear, prismatic shear, and near-fault offset structures
support_plan.eval_family: held-out basal/prismatic path points and offset relaxations
support_plan.leakage_guard: hash species, cell, fractional positions, shear path id, path coordinate, and seed

allowed_correction_coordinates: soft shear complement basis from subspace diagnostics
stiff_axes_to_preserve: hcp c/a ratio outside the applied shear protocol
refusal_triggers: cell collapse or nonphysical c/a drift
path coordinate leaves the locked shear protocol

phase_0_preflight: Lock one basal and one prismatic path protocol.
Generate local hcp Mg offset structures and baseline scores.
Run projected ribbon in shadow mode and verify stiff-axis diagnostics.
phase_1_local_pair: Run paired baseline and Distill Accuracy path points on top local MLIPs.
Reject policies that improve endpoint energy while worsening path shape.
Publish path-wise intervention and refusal traces.
phase_2_cloud: Canary one shear path and one relaxation path before broad campaign.
Keep acceleration disabled until accuracy path shape is stable.

viewer_contract.scene_rule: Render measured hcp cell motion, applied shear path coordinate, slip-plane guide, force/stress overlays, and intervention events only from scored artifacts.

science_contract.primary_question: Can Distill improve a complex Li-ion migration or site-recovery task while refusing chemically implausible jumps and preserving the host lattice?

required_sources: locked LiFePO4 source structure and license
published DFT Li migration barrier or path-energy reference
blocked_until_locked: shadow run using unlabeled migration coordinate

support_plan.support_family: non-overlapping LiFePO4 site perturbations, short path images, host-lattice finite perturbations, and chemically valid Li moves
support_plan.eval_family: held-out Li-channel migration frames and site occupancy recovery cases
support_plan.leakage_guard: hash species, oxidation-state metadata if present, site labels, path image id, fractional positions, and seed

allowed_correction_coordinates: soft Li-channel complement basis from subspace diagnostics
refusal_triggers: host framework distortion exceeds stiff-axis budget
backend lacks species support or returns nonfinite values

phase_0_preflight: Lock LiFePO4 structure, source license, and migration coordinate.
Verify each backend supports the required species without fallback.
Build the channel graph and site labels before running Distill.
phase_1_local_pair: Run one short baseline path and one shadow Distill pass.
Promote to active local correction only if no nonphysical jump appears.
Score site occupancy, path energy, force stability, and host drift.
phase_2_cloud: Run a tiny Cloud Run canary only after local pair and viewer artifact validation.
Keep classical baselines marked not_applicable unless a scientifically valid potential is selected.

viewer_contract.scene_rule: Render measured Li positions through a locked channel graph with host-lattice drift and site occupancy overlays only after the structure and path reference are locked.
